features.claim: The agent supports a structured three-phase workflow — exploration, planning, and execution — for complex code changes.
features.name: Explore, plan, then execute
subfeatures: Parallel subagents: The primary agent can spawn concurrent background subagents for large-scale, multi-file refactoring tasks, managed autonomously.

features.claim: The agent can run local test commands (e.g. unit tests, build scripts) after making code changes and iterate automatically.
features.name: Verification loops

features.claim: Configurable permission modes (request-review, proceed-in-sandbox, strict) control how autonomously the agent executes work.
features.name: Structured permissions

features.claim: The agent supports rewinding, forking, and non-interactive execution of conversation sessions.
features.name: Session management
subfeatures: Session history rewind: The /rewind (or /undo) command rolls back the conversation thread to a previous stable checkpoint without discarding the work.
Session forking: The /fork command spins up a duplicate parallel session so users can test speculative code changes and swap back to the original.
Non-interactive (headless) mode: A one-shot prompt flag (-p) lets the CLI run agent queries non-interactively, enabling integration into shell pipelines.

features.claim: Users can enrich the agent prompt with workspace file path autocomplete and visual media.
features.name: Prompt context inputs
subfeatures: Interactive Path Suggestion: Typing '@' in the prompt box triggers an overlay that auto-completes workspace file paths directly into the prompt.
Attaching visual evidence: Users can paste screenshots or video recordings into the prompt box and the agent will consult the media to diagnose visual issues.

features.claim: A GEMINI.md or AGENTS.md file at the workspace root is automatically parsed by the agent on startup to enforce project-specific rules.
features.name: Codebase rule file
